title: Clearance Delivery
description: Obtain your IFR clearance before pushback. Learn the CRAFT acronym.

content.definition: The acronym for IFR clearance items: Clearance limit, Route, Altitude, Frequency, Transponder (squawk).
content.example_atc: EZY123, cleared to London Heathrow via BOGNA, MERLU, MATCH, flight planned route. Climb via SID to FL150. Squawk 2341.
content.example_response: Cleared to London Heathrow via BOGNA MERLU MATCH, climb via SID to FL150, squawk 2341, EZY123.

content.definition: Request to push the aircraft back from the gate and start engines.
content.example_atc: EZY123, push and start approved, face south.
content.example_response: Push and start approved, face south, EZY123.

content.atc_text: EZY123, cleared to London Heathrow via flight planned route. Climb initially to FL70, expect FL350 ten minutes after departure. Departure frequency 119.72. Squawk 2341.
content.question: What is the initial climb altitude?
content.explanation: The clearance says 'climb initially to FL70'. FL350 is the expected cruise level, not the initial climb.

content.prompt: EZY123, cleared to London Heathrow via flight planned route. Climb initially to _____, squawk _____.
content.context: ATC is issuing your IFR departure clearance.

content.atc_text: EZY123, push and start approved, face south.
content.correct_response: Push and start approved, face south, EZY123.
content.hint: Readback the push direction and your callsign.
acceptable_variants: Approved push and start facing south, EZY123.
